name: Kevin Weil
summary: Announced ChatGPT 26, a program for standout students and recent grads that offers access to OpenAI HQ, frontier tooling, and a $10K grant, framed around young researchers already using AI to accelerate science.

name: Nan Yu
summary: Shared a concrete coding quality moment: Codex caught a prompt line that indirectly referenced a feature hidden behind a separate feature flag, suggesting AI review is starting to beat many human reviewers on subtle product-context issues.

name: Thariq
summary: Highlighted how fast Anthropic is shipping computer-use capabilities, noting the team acquired a computer-use company and then launched the feature four weeks later; also pointed users to Claude Code Desktop docs.

name: Google Labs
summary: Said the Doppl virtual try-on app will shut down on April 30, 2026, with its learnings folded into Google Shopping and image results, a good reminder that Labs products are experiments whose best ideas may graduate rather than survive as standalone apps.

name: Alex Albert
summary: Pointed to a physicist guest post claiming Opus 4.5 performs around the level of a second-year grad student and accelerated research by 10x, reinforcing the narrative that frontier models are becoming serious scientific copilots.

name: Aaron Levie
summary: Argued that computer use plus on-the-fly code generation are the core primitives agents need for real enterprise knowledge work, because useful automation requires traversing apps, APIs, workflows, and messy business data end to end.

name: Garry Tan
summary: Reported shipping a 2,400-line PR into YC's 1.84M-line internal codebase with help from GStack/Conductor, arguing that AI coding tools are not just for greenfield solo projects but can now meaningfully tackle giant legacy systems too.

name: Matt Turck
summary: Observed that tools like Claude Code are collapsing the old need to spend years searching for a technical cofounder before building, a succinct investor read on how agentic coding is shifting startup formation.

name: Peter Steinberger
summary: Described an AI-assisted PR review workflow where Codex surfaces issues first, but human reviewers still need to judge whether the issue is clearly framed and whether the proposed fix improves long-term maintainability rather than patching locally.

name: Dan Shipper
summary: Made the practical point that vibe coding is not the same thing as vibe fixing: generating code is getting easier fast, but debugging and repairing systems under time pressure remains a distinct skill.

name: Sam Altman
summary: Stepped off Helion's board while keeping a financial interest, saying the move simplifies governance as OpenAI and Helion explore working together at meaningful scale, which hints at deeper AI-energy infrastructure coordination ahead.

name: Claude
summary: Showed a paired mobile-desktop workflow where users can assign tasks from their phone, let Claude use connected apps first, and escalate to direct computer control with permission when no integration exists, pushing the assistant closer to a persistent agent.
